1	import type {
2	  API,
3	  ASTPath,
4	  FileInfo,
5	  FunctionDeclaration,
6	  Collection,
7	  Node,
8	  ImportSpecifier,
9	  Identifier,
10	} from 'jscodeshift'
11	import { createParserFromPath } from '../lib/parser'
12	
13	const GEO = 'geo'
14	const IP = 'ip'
15	const GEOLOCATION = 'geolocation'
16	const IP_ADDRESS = 'ipAddress'
17	const GEO_TYPE = 'Geo'
18	
19	export default function (file: FileInfo, _api: API) {
20	  const j = createParserFromPath(file.path)
21	  const root = j(file.source)
22	
23	  if (!root.length) {
24	    return file.source
25	  }
26	
27	  const nextReqType = root
28	    .find(j.FunctionDeclaration)
29	    .find(j.Identifier, (id) => {
30	      if (id.typeAnnotation?.type !== 'TSTypeAnnotation') {
31	        return false
32	      }
33	
34	      const typeAnn = id.typeAnnotation.typeAnnotation
35	      return (
36	        typeAnn.type === 'TSTypeReference' &&
37	        typeAnn.typeName.type === 'Identifier' &&
38	        typeAnn.typeName.name === 'NextRequest'
39	      )
40	    })
41	
42	  const vercelFuncImports = root.find(j.ImportDeclaration, {
43	    source: {
44	      value: '@vercel/functions',
45	    },
46	  })
47	
48	  const vercelFuncImportSpecifiers = vercelFuncImports
49	    .find(j.ImportSpecifier)
50	    .nodes()
51	
52	  const vercelFuncImportNames = new Set(
53	    vercelFuncImportSpecifiers.map((node) => node.imported.name)
54	  )
55	
56	  const hasGeolocation = vercelFuncImportNames.has(GEOLOCATION)
57	  const hasIpAddress = vercelFuncImportNames.has(IP_ADDRESS)
58	  const hasGeoType = vercelFuncImportNames.has(GEO_TYPE)
59	
60	  let identifierNames = new Set<string>()
61	
62	  // if all identifiers are already imported, we don't need to create
63	  // a unique identifier for them, therefore we don't look for all
64	  // identifier names in the file
65	  if (!hasGeolocation || !hasIpAddress || !hasGeoType) {
66	    const allIdentifiers = root.find(j.Identifier).nodes()
67	    identifierNames = new Set(allIdentifiers.map((node) => node.name))
68	  }
69	
70	  let geoIdentifier = hasGeolocation
71	    ? getExistingIdentifier(vercelFuncImportSpecifiers, GEOLOCATION)
72	    : getUniqueIdentifier(identifierNames, GEOLOCATION)
73	
74	  let ipIdentifier = hasIpAddress
75	    ? getExistingIdentifier(vercelFuncImportSpecifiers, IP_ADDRESS)
76	    : getUniqueIdentifier(identifierNames, IP_ADDRESS)
77	
78	  let geoTypeIdentifier = hasGeoType
79	    ? getExistingIdentifier(vercelFuncImportSpecifiers, GEO_TYPE)
80	    : getUniqueIdentifier(identifierNames, GEO_TYPE)
81	
82	  let { needImportGeolocation, needImportIpAddress } = replaceGeoIpValues(
83	    j,
84	    nextReqType,
85	    geoIdentifier,
86	    ipIdentifier
87	  )
88	  let { needImportGeoType, hasChangedIpType } = replaceGeoIpTypes(
89	    j,
90	    root,
91	    geoTypeIdentifier
92	  )
93	
94	  let needChanges =
95	    needImportGeolocation ||
96	    needImportIpAddress ||
97	    needImportGeoType ||
98	    hasChangedIpType
99	
100	  if (!needChanges) {
101	    return file.source
102	  }
103	
104	  // Even if there was a change above, if there's an existing import,
105	  // we don't need to import them again.
106	  needImportGeolocation = !hasGeolocation && needImportGeolocation
107	  needImportIpAddress = !hasIpAddress && needImportIpAddress
108	  needImportGeoType = !hasGeoType && needImportGeoType
109	
110	  insertImportDeclarations(
111	    j,
112	    root,
113	    vercelFuncImports,
114	    needImportGeolocation,
115	    needImportIpAddress,
116	    needImportGeoType,
117	    geoIdentifier,
118	    ipIdentifier,
119	    geoTypeIdentifier
120	  )
121	  return root.toSource()
122	}

124	/**
125	 * Returns an existing identifier from the Vercel functions import declaration.
126	 */
127	function getExistingIdentifier(
128	  vercelFuncImportSpecifiers: ImportSpecifier[],
129	  identifier: string
130	) {
131	  const existingIdentifier = vercelFuncImportSpecifiers.find(
132	    (node) => node.imported.name === identifier
133	  )
134	
135	  return (
136	    existingIdentifier?.local?.name ||
137	    existingIdentifier.imported.name ||
138	    identifier
139	  )
140	}
141	
142	/**
143	 * Returns a unique identifier by adding a suffix to the original identifier
144	 * if it already exists in the set.
145	 */
146	function getUniqueIdentifier(identifierNames: Set<string>, identifier: string) {
147	  let suffix = 1
148	  let uniqueIdentifier = identifier
149	  while (identifierNames.has(uniqueIdentifier)) {
150	    uniqueIdentifier = `${identifier}${suffix}`
151	    suffix++
152	  }
153	  return uniqueIdentifier
154	}

156	/**
157	 * Replaces accessors `geo` and `ip` of NextRequest with corresponding
158	 * function calls from `@vercel/functions`.
159	 *
160	 * Creates new variable declarations for destructuring assignments.
161	 */
162	function replaceGeoIpValues(
163	  j: API['jscodeshift'],
164	  nextReqType: Collection<Identifier>,
165	  geoIdentifier: string,
166	  ipIdentifier: string
167	): {
168	  needImportGeolocation: boolean
169	  needImportIpAddress: boolean
170	} {
171	  let needImportGeolocation = false
172	  let needImportIpAddress = false
173	
174	  for (const nextReqPath of nextReqType.paths()) {
175	    const fnPath: ASTPath<FunctionDeclaration> =
176	      nextReqPath.parentPath.parentPath
177	    const fn = j(fnPath)
178	    const blockStatement = fn.find(j.BlockStatement)
179	    const varDeclarators = fn.find(j.VariableDeclarator)
180	
181	    // req.geo, req.ip
182	    const geoAccesses = blockStatement.find(
183	      j.MemberExpression,
184	      (me) =>
185	        me.object.type === 'Identifier' &&
186	        me.object.name === nextReqPath.node.name &&
187	        me.property.type === 'Identifier' &&
188	        me.property.name === GEO
189	    )
190	    const ipAccesses = blockStatement.find(
191	      j.MemberExpression,
192	      (me) =>
193	        me.object.type === 'Identifier' &&
194	        me.object.name === nextReqPath.node.name &&
195	        me.property.type === 'Identifier' &&
196	        me.property.name === IP
197	    )
198	
199	    // var { geo, ip } = req
200	    const geoDestructuring = varDeclarators.filter(
201	      (path) =>
202	        path.node.id.type === 'ObjectPattern' &&
203	        path.node.id.properties.some(
204	          (prop) =>
205	            prop.type === 'ObjectProperty' &&
206	            prop.key.type === 'Identifier' &&
207	            prop.key.name === GEO
208	        )
209	    )
210	    const ipDestructuring = varDeclarators.filter(
211	      (path) =>
212	        path.node.id.type === 'ObjectPattern' &&
213	        path.node.id.properties.some(
214	          (prop) =>
215	            prop.type === 'ObjectProperty' &&
216	            prop.key.type === 'Identifier' &&
217	            prop.key.name === IP
218	        )
219	    )
220	
221	    // geolocation(req), ipAddress(req)
222	    const geoCall = j.callExpression(j.identifier(geoIdentifier), [
223	      {
224	        ...nextReqPath.node,
225	        typeAnnotation: null,
226	      },
227	    ])
228	    const ipCall = j.callExpression(j.identifier(ipIdentifier), [
229	      {
230	        ...nextReqPath.node,
231	        typeAnnotation: null,
232	      },
233	    ])
234	
235	    geoAccesses.replaceWith(geoCall)
236	    ipAccesses.replaceWith(ipCall)
237	
238	    /**
239	     * For each destructuring assignment, we create a new variable
240	     * declaration and insert it after the current block statement.
241	     *
242	     * Before:
243	     *
244	     * ```
245	     * const { buildId, geo, ip } = req;
246	     * ```
247	     *
248	     * After:
249	     *
250	     * ```
251	     * const { buildId } = req;
252	     * const geo = geolocation(req);
253	     * const ip = ipAddress(req);
254	     * ```
255	     */
256	    geoDestructuring.forEach((path) => {
257	      if (path.node.id.type === 'ObjectPattern') {
258	        const properties = path.node.id.properties
259	        const geoProperty = properties.find(
260	          (prop) =>
261	            prop.type === 'ObjectProperty' &&
262	            prop.key.type === 'Identifier' &&
263	            prop.key.name === GEO
264	        )
265	
266	        const otherProperties = properties.filter(
267	          (prop) => prop !== geoProperty
268	        )
269	
270	        const geoDeclaration = j.variableDeclaration('const', [
271	          j.variableDeclarator(
272	            j.identifier(
273	              // Use alias from destructuring (if present) to retain references:
274	              // const { geo: geoAlias } = req; -> const geoAlias = geolocation(req);
275	              // This prevents errors from undeclared variables.
276	              geoProperty.type === 'ObjectProperty' &&
277	                geoProperty.value.type === 'Identifier'
278	                ? geoProperty.value.name
279	                : GEO
280	            ),
281	            geoCall
282	          ),
283	        ])
284	
285	        path.node.id.properties = otherProperties
286	        path.parent.insertAfter(geoDeclaration)
287	      }
288	    })
289	    ipDestructuring.forEach((path) => {
290	      if (path.node.id.type === 'ObjectPattern') {
291	        const properties = path.node.id.properties
292	        const ipProperty = properties.find(
293	          (prop) =>
294	            prop.type === 'ObjectProperty' &&
295	            prop.key.type === 'Identifier' &&
296	            prop.key.name === IP
297	        )
298	        const otherProperties = properties.filter((prop) => prop !== ipProperty)
299	
300	        const ipDeclaration = j.variableDeclaration('const', [
301	          j.variableDeclarator(
302	            j.identifier(
303	              // Use alias from destructuring (if present) to retain references:
304	              // const { ip: ipAlias } = req; -> const ipAlias = ipAddress(req);
305	              // This prevents errors from undeclared variables.
306	              ipProperty.type === 'ObjectProperty' &&
307	                ipProperty.value.type === 'Identifier'
308	                ? ipProperty.value.name
309	                : IP
310	            ),
311	            ipCall
312	          ),
313	        ])
314	
315	        path.node.id.properties = otherProperties
316	        path.parent.insertAfter(ipDeclaration)
317	      }
318	    })
319	
320	    needImportGeolocation =
321	      needImportGeolocation ||
322	      geoAccesses.length > 0 ||
323	      geoDestructuring.length > 0
324	    needImportIpAddress =
325	      needImportIpAddress || ipAccesses.length > 0 || ipDestructuring.length > 0
326	  }
327	
328	  return {
329	    needImportGeolocation,
330	    needImportIpAddress,
331	  }
332	}

334	/**
335	 * Replaces the types of `NextRequest["geo"]` and `NextRequest["ip"]` with
336	 * corresponding types from `@vercel/functions`.
337	 */
338	function replaceGeoIpTypes(
339	  j: API['jscodeshift'],
340	  root: Collection<Node>,
341	  geoTypeIdentifier: string
342	): {
343	  needImportGeoType: boolean
344	  hasChangedIpType: boolean
345	} {
346	  let needImportGeoType = false
347	  let hasChangedIpType = false
348	
349	  // get the type of NextRequest that has accessed for ip and geo
350	  // NextRequest['geo'], NextRequest['ip']
351	  const nextReqGeoType = root.find(
352	    j.TSIndexedAccessType,
353	    (tsIndexedAccessType) => {
354	      return (
355	        tsIndexedAccessType.objectType.type === 'TSTypeReference' &&
356	        tsIndexedAccessType.objectType.typeName.type === 'Identifier' &&
357	        tsIndexedAccessType.objectType.typeName.name === 'NextRequest' &&
358	        tsIndexedAccessType.indexType.type === 'TSLiteralType' &&
359	        tsIndexedAccessType.indexType.literal.type === 'StringLiteral' &&
360	        tsIndexedAccessType.indexType.literal.value === GEO
361	      )
362	    }
363	  )
364	  const nextReqIpType = root.find(
365	    j.TSIndexedAccessType,
366	    (tsIndexedAccessType) => {
367	      return (
368	        tsIndexedAccessType.objectType.type === 'TSTypeReference' &&
369	        tsIndexedAccessType.objectType.typeName.type === 'Identifier' &&
370	        tsIndexedAccessType.objectType.typeName.name === 'NextRequest' &&
371	        tsIndexedAccessType.indexType.type === 'TSLiteralType' &&
372	        tsIndexedAccessType.indexType.literal.type === 'StringLiteral' &&
373	        tsIndexedAccessType.indexType.literal.value === IP
374	      )
375	    }
376	  )
377	
378	  if (nextReqGeoType.length > 0) {
379	    needImportGeoType = true
380	
381	    // replace with type Geo
382	    nextReqGeoType.replaceWith(j.identifier(geoTypeIdentifier))
383	  }
384	
385	  if (nextReqIpType.length > 0) {
386	    hasChangedIpType = true
387	
388	    // replace with type string | undefined
389	    nextReqIpType.replaceWith(
390	      j.tsUnionType([j.tsStringKeyword(), j.tsUndefinedKeyword()])
391	    )
392	  }
393	
394	  return {
395	    needImportGeoType,
396	    hasChangedIpType,
397	  }
398	}
399	
400	/**
401	 * Inserts import declarations from `"@vercel/functions"`.
402	 *
403	 * For the `Geo` type that needs to be imported to replace `NextRequest["geo"]`,
404	 * if it is the only import needed, we import it as a type.
405	 *
406	 * ```ts
407	 * import type { Geo } from "@vercel/functions";
408	 * ```
409	 *
410	 * Otherwise, we import it as an inline type import.
411	 *
412	 * ```ts
413	 * import { type Geo, ... } from "@vercel/functions";
414	 * ```
415	 */
416	function insertImportDeclarations(
417	  j: API['jscodeshift'],
418	  root: Collection<Node>,
419	  vercelFuncImports: Collection<Node>,
420	  needImportGeolocation: boolean,
421	  needImportIpAddress: boolean,
422	  needImportGeoType: boolean,
423	  geoIdentifier: string,
424	  ipIdentifier: string,
425	  geoTypeIdentifier: string
426	): void {
427	  // No need inserting import.
428	  if (!needImportGeolocation && !needImportIpAddress && !needImportGeoType) {
429	    return
430	  }
431

16	/**
17	 * Creates an AbortController tied to the closing of a ServerResponse (or other
18	 * appropriate Writable).
19	 *
20	 * If the `close` event is fired before the `finish` event, then we'll send the
21	 * `abort` signal.
22	 */
23	export function createAbortController(response: Writable): AbortController {
24	  const controller = new AbortController()
25	
26	  // If `finish` fires first, then `res.end()` has been called and the close is
27	  // just us finishing the stream on our side. If `close` fires first, then we
28	  // know the client disconnected before we finished.
29	  response.once('close', () => {
30	    if (response.writableFinished) return
31	
32	    controller.abort(new ResponseAborted())
33	  })
34	
35	  return controller
36	}

38	/**
39	 * Creates an AbortSignal tied to the closing of a ServerResponse (or other
40	 * appropriate Writable).
41	 *
42	 * This cannot be done with the request (IncomingMessage or Readable) because
43	 * the `abort` event will not fire if to data has been fully read (because that
44	 * will "close" the readable stream and nothing fires after that).
45	 */

47	  const { errored, destroyed } = response
48	  if (errored || destroyed) {
49	    return AbortSignal.abort(errored ?? new ResponseAborted())
50	  }
51	
52	  const { signal } = createAbortController(response)
53	  return signal
54	}

## Complete Analysis of Next.js Entry Point Patterns

I've analyzed the requested files and additional entry points. Here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. **packages/next/server.js** (Public CommonJS Entry Point)

**Full Contents:**
```javascript
const serverExports = {
  NextRequest: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/request')
    .NextRequest,
  NextResponse: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/response')
    .NextResponse,
  ImageResponse: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/image-response')
    .ImageResponse,
  userAgentFromString: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/user-agent')
    .userAgentFromString,
  userAgent: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/user-agent')
    .userAgent,
  URLPattern: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/url-pattern')
    .URLPattern,
  after: require('next/dist/server/after').after,
  connection: require('next/dist/server/request/connection').connection,
}

// https://nodejs.org/api/esm.html#commonjs-namespaces
// When importing CommonJS modules, the module.exports object is provided as the default export
module.exports = serverExports

// make import { xxx } from 'next/server' work
exports.NextRequest = serverExports.NextRequest
exports.NextResponse = serverExports.NextResponse
exports.ImageResponse = serverExports.ImageResponse
exports.userAgentFromString = serverExports.userAgentFromString
exports.userAgent = serverExports.userAgent
exports.URLPattern = serverExports.URLPattern
exports.after = serverExports.after
exports.connection = serverExports.connection
```

**Key Pattern:** Creates a named exports object, assigns it to `module.exports` for default import, then re-exports each named export individually on `exports` object for named imports.

---

### 2. **packages/next/server.d.ts** (TypeScript Definitions)

**Full Contents:**
```typescript
import type { AsyncLocalStorage as NodeAsyncLocalStorage } from 'async_hooks'

declare global {
  var AsyncLocalStorage: typeof NodeAsyncLocalStorage
}

export { NextFetchEvent } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/fetch-event'
export { NextRequest } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/request'
export { NextResponse } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/response'
export {
  NextMiddleware,
  MiddlewareConfig,
  NextProxy,
  ProxyConfig,
} from 'next/dist/server/web/types'
export { userAgentFromString } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/user-agent'
export { userAgent } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/user-agent'
export { URLPattern } from 'next/dist/compiled/@edge-runtime/primitives/url'
export { ImageResponse } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/image-response'
export type { ImageResponseOptions } from 'next/dist/compiled/@vercel/og/types'
export { after } from 'next/dist/server/after'

```

**Key Pattern:** Pure type-level re-exports from implementation modules. Uses `export { X } from 'path'` pattern for transparent type re-exporting. Also sets up a global type augmentation for `AsyncLocalStorage`.

---

### 3. **packages/next/src/server/web/spec-extension/user-agent.ts** (Implementation)

**Full Contents:**
```typescript
import parseua from 'next/dist/compiled/ua-parser-js'

interface UserAgent {
  isBot: boolean
  ua: string
  browser: {
    name?: string
    version?: string
    major?: string
  }
  device: {
    model?: string
    type?: string
    vendor?: string
  }
  engine: {
    name?: string
    version?: string
  }
  os: {
    name?: string
    version?: string
  }
  cpu: {
    architecture?: string
  }
}

export function isBot(input: string): boolean {
  return /Googlebot|Mediapartners-Google|AdsBot-Google|googleweblight|Storebot-Google|Google-PageRenderer|Google-InspectionTool|Bingbot|BingPreview|Slurp|DuckDuckBot|baiduspider|yandex|sogou|LinkedInBot|bitlybot|tumblr|vkShare|quora link preview|facebookexternalhit|facebookcatalog|Twitterbot|applebot|redditbot|Slackbot|Discordbot|WhatsApp|SkypeUriPreview|ia_archiver|GPTBot/i.test(
    input
  )
}

export function userAgentFromString(input: string | undefined): UserAgent {
  return {
    ...parseua(input),
    isBot: input === undefined ? false : isBot(input),
  }
}

export function userAgent({ headers }: { headers: Headers }): UserAgent {
  return userAgentFromString(headers.get('user-agent') || undefined)
}
```

**Key Pattern:** Defines a TypeScript interface and exports functions. The `userAgentFromString` function delegates to `ua-parser-js` via spreading, then adds an `isBot` property. The `userAgent` function extracts the UA string from headers.

---

### Additional Entry Point Patterns (Similar Structure)

#### **packages/next/headers.js**
```javascript
module.exports.cookies = require('./dist/server/request/cookies').cookies
module.exports.headers = require('./dist/server/request/headers').headers

```

#### **packages/next/headers.d.ts**
```typescript

export * from './dist/server/request/headers'
export * from './dist/server/request/draft-mode'
```

#### **packages/next/navigation.js**
```javascript

```

#### **packages/next/navigation.d.ts**
```typescript
export * from './dist/client/components/navigation'
```

#### **packages/next/cache.js**
```javascript
let cacheExports

if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME === '') {
  // Client-side stubs...
} else {
  // Server-side implementations...
}

cacheExports.unstable_cacheLife = unstable_cacheLife
// ... exports pattern similar to server.js
```

#### **Simple Entry Points**
- **app.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/pages/_app')`
- **client.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/client/index')`
- **image.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/shared/lib/image-external')`
- **link.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/client/link')`

---

## Pattern Summary

**Three main patterns exist:**

1. **Simple Pass-through** (`app.js`, `client.js`, `image.js`, `link.js`):
   - JS: `module.exports = require('./dist/...')`
   - TS: N/A (single export only)

2. **Multi-Export with Dual Assignment** (`server.js`, `cache.js`):
   - JS: Create object, assign to `module.exports`, then enumerate each export on `exports`
   - Purpose: Support both default (`import x from 'next/server'`) and named imports (`import { x } from 'next/server'`)

3. **Multi-Export with Star Re-export** (`headers.js`, `navigation.js`):
   - JS: Individual `module.exports.x = require(...)`
   - TS: `export * from './dist/...'`
   - Simpler variant for multi-export modules

**TypeScript Definition Patterns:**
- `server.d.ts`: Explicit individual re-exports + type augmentation
- `headers.d.ts`: Star re-exports
- `navigation.d.ts`: Star re-exports

The JS/TS pairs always mirror each other in semantics, with JS using CommonJS requires and TypeScript using ES6 re-exports.

**Key Pattern:** Defines a TypeScript interface and exports functions. The `userAgentFromString` function delegates to `ua-parser-js` via spreading, then adds an `isBot` property. The `userAgent` function extracts the UA string from headers.

---

### Additional Entry Point Patterns (Similar Structure)

#### **packages/next/headers.js**
```javascript
module.exports.cookies = require('./dist/server/request/cookies').cookies
module.exports.headers = require('./dist/server/request/headers').headers
module.exports.draftMode = require('./dist/server/request/draft-mode').draftMode
```

#### **packages/next/headers.d.ts**
```typescript
export * from './dist/server/request/cookies'
export * from './dist/server/request/headers'
export * from './dist/server/request/draft-mode'
```

#### **packages/next/navigation.js**
```javascript
module.exports = require('./dist/client/components/navigation')
```

#### **packages/next/navigation.d.ts**
```typescript
export * from './dist/client/components/navigation'
```

#### **packages/next/cache.js**
```javascript
let cacheExports

if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME === '') {
  // Client-side stubs...
} else {
  // Server-side implementations...
}

cacheExports.unstable_cacheLife = unstable_cacheLife
// ... exports pattern similar to server.js
```

#### **Simple Entry Points**
- **app.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/pages/_app')`
- **client.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/client/index')`
- **image.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/shared/lib/image-external')`
- **link.js**: `module.exports = require('./dist/client/link')`

---

## Pattern Summary

**Three main patterns exist:**

1. **Simple Pass-through** (`app.js`, `client.js`, `image.js`, `link.js`):
   - JS: `module.exports = require('./dist/...')`
   - TS: N/A (single export only)

2. **Multi-Export with Dual Assignment** (`server.js`, `cache.js`):
   - JS: Create object, assign to `module.exports`, then enumerate each export on `exports`
   - Purpose: Support both default (`import x from 'next/server'`) and named imports (`import { x } from 'next/server'`)

3. **Multi-Export with Star Re-export** (`headers.js`, `navigation.js`):
   - JS: Individual `module.exports.x = require(...)`
   - TS: `export * from './dist/...'`
   - Simpler variant for multi-export modules

**TypeScript Definition Patterns:**
- `server.d.ts`: Explicit individual re-exports + type augmentation
- `headers.d.ts`: Star re-exports
- `navigation.d.ts`: Star re-exports

The JS/TS pairs always mirror each other in semantics, with JS using CommonJS requires and TypeScript using ES6 re-exports.

**Found in Codemod Transforms:**

- `/packages/next-codemod/transforms/next-request-geo-ip.ts`

**Key Patterns Identified:**

The codemod handles replacing deprecated `NextRequest.geo` and `NextRequest.ip` properties with function calls from `@vercel/functions`:

From the codemod source (lines 13-17):
```typescript
const GEO = 'geo'
const IP = 'ip'
const GEOLOCATION = 'geolocation'
const IP_ADDRESS = 'ipAddress'
const GEO_TYPE = 'Geo'
```

**What Gets Replaced:**
- `req.geo` → `geolocation(req)` function call
- `req.ip` → `ipAddress(req)` function call
- Type `NextRequest['geo']` → Type `Geo`
- Type `NextRequest['ip']` → Type `string | undefined`

**Example from test fixture** (`geo-type-import.output.ts`):
```typescript
import { geolocation, ipAddress, type Geo } from "@vercel/functions";

export function GET(request: NextRequest) {
  const geo = geolocation(request) as Geo
  const ip = ipAddress(request)
  return NextResponse.json({ geo, ip })
}
```

- `city`
- `country`
- `region`
- `latitude`
- `longitude`
- `timezone`

- 20 files found containing "geo" related patterns in codemod test fixtures
- Files: `/packages/next-codemod/transforms/__testfixtures__/next-request-geo-ip/*`

# Plan: Add `next/geo` Utility Module

## Context

Next.js previously had geolocation support via `NextRequest.geo` which was deprecated and migrated to `@vercel/functions`. This task creates a new first-party `next/geo` utility module that provides geolocation helpers for middleware and edge functions, following the exact same entry-point pattern used by `next/server` (which exposes `userAgent`/`userAgentFromString`).

## Files to Create

### 1. Core Implementation: `packages/next/src/server/web/spec-extension/geo.ts`

Create the implementation file following the pattern of `user-agent.ts` (same directory).

```typescript
export interface Geo {
  city?: string
  country?: string
  countryCode?: string
  region?: string
  latitude?: string
  longitude?: string
  timezone?: string
}

const COUNTRY_NAMES: Record<string, string> = {
  US: 'United States',
  GB: 'United Kingdom',
  DE: 'Germany',
  FR: 'France',
  JP: 'Japan',
  CN: 'China',
  IN: 'India',
  BR: 'Brazil',
  AU: 'Australia',
  CA: 'Canada',
}

function extractGeoFromHeaders(headers: Headers): Geo {
  const countryCode =
    headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country') ||
    headers.get('cf-ipcountry') ||
    undefined

  return {
    city: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-city') || undefined,
    countryCode: countryCode || undefined,
    country: countryCode ? COUNTRY_NAMES[countryCode.toUpperCase()] : undefined,
    region: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country-region') || undefined,
    latitude: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-latitude') || undefined,
    longitude: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-longitude') || undefined,
    timezone: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-timezone') || undefined,
  }
}

export function geo({ headers }: { headers: Headers }): Geo {
  return extractGeoFromHeaders(headers)
}

export function geoFromHeaders(headers: Headers): Geo {
  return extractGeoFromHeaders(headers)
}

export function isInRegion(geoData: Geo, regions: string[]): boolean {
  if (!geoData.countryCode) return false
  const upperCode = geoData.countryCode.toUpperCase()
  return regions.some((r) => r.toUpperCase() === upperCode)
}

export function geoDistance(
  lat1: string,
  lon1: string,
  lat2: string,
  lon2: string
): number {
  const R = 6371 // Earth's radius in km
  const toRad = (deg: number) => (deg * Math.PI) / 180
  const dLat = toRad(parseFloat(lat2) - parseFloat(lat1))
  const dLon = toRad(parseFloat(lon2) - parseFloat(lon1))
  const a =
    Math.sin(dLat / 2) * Math.sin(dLat / 2) +
    Math.cos(toRad(parseFloat(lat1))) *
      Math.cos(toRad(parseFloat(lat2))) *
      Math.sin(dLon / 2) *
      Math.sin(dLon / 2)
  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a))
  return R * c
}
```

**Key design decisions:**
- Private `extractGeoFromHeaders` shared between `geo()` and `geoFromHeaders()` to avoid duplication
- `headers.get()` returns `null` for missing headers; we convert to `undefined` with `|| undefined`
- `country` is derived from `countryCode` via the `COUNTRY_NAMES` map (uppercase lookup)
- Cloudflare `cf-ipcountry` is a fallback for `countryCode` only (Vercel header checked first)
- `isInRegion` does case-insensitive comparison
- `geoDistance` uses Haversine formula (standard approach for great-circle distance)

### 2. Public Entry Point: `packages/next/geo.js`

Follow the `server.js` dual-export pattern exactly:

```javascript
const geoExports = {
  geo: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo').geo,
  geoFromHeaders: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo')
    .geoFromHeaders,
  isInRegion: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo').isInRegion,
  geoDistance: require('next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo').geoDistance,
}

// https://nodejs.org/api/esm.html#commonjs-namespaces
module.exports = geoExports

// make import { xxx } from 'next/geo' work
exports.geo = geoExports.geo
exports.geoFromHeaders = geoExports.geoFromHeaders
exports.isInRegion = geoExports.isInRegion
exports.geoDistance = geoExports.geoDistance
```

### 3. Type Definitions: `packages/next/geo.d.ts`

Follow the `server.d.ts` explicit re-export pattern:

```typescript
export { geo } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo'
export { geoFromHeaders } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo'
export { isInRegion } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo'
export { geoDistance } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo'
export type { Geo } from 'next/dist/server/web/spec-extension/geo'
```

### 4. Test File: `packages/next/src/server/web/spec-extension/geo.test.ts`

Follow the Jest pattern used by nearby tests (e.g., `adapters/headers.test.ts`):

```typescript

describe('geo', () => {
  // Test geo() with Vercel headers

  // Test geoDistance() NY↔London ~5570km (±50km tolerance)
  // Test missing headers → undefined fields
  // Test country name lookup
})
```

Uses `describe`/`it`/`expect` (Jest globals), `new Headers()` for constructing test headers.

## File to Modify

### 5. `packages/next/package.json` — `files` array

Add `"geo.js"` and `"geo.d.ts"` after `"form.d.ts"` (line 43), placing `geo` alphabetically between `form` and `head`:

  city?: string
  country?: string
  countryCode?: string
  region?: string
  latitude?: string
  longitude?: string
  timezone?: string
}

  US: 'United States',
  GB: 'United Kingdom',
  DE: 'Germany',
  FR: 'France',
  JP: 'Japan',
  CN: 'China',
  IN: 'India',
  BR: 'Brazil',
  AU: 'Australia',
  CA: 'Canada',
}

function extractGeoFromHeaders(headers: Headers): Geo {
  const countryCode =
    headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country') ||
    headers.get('cf-ipcountry') ||
    undefined

  return {
    city: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-city') || undefined,
    countryCode: countryCode || undefined,

    region: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country-region') || undefined,
    latitude: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-latitude') || undefined,
    longitude: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-longitude') || undefined,
    timezone: headers.get('x-vercel-ip-timezone') || undefined,

1	/**
2	 * Geolocation utilities for Next.js middleware and edge functions.
3	 *
4	 * Extracts geolocation data from common CDN/proxy headers
5	 * (Vercel, Cloudflare) and provides helpers for region checks
6	 * and distance calculations.
7	 */
8	
9	export interface Geo {
10	  city?: string
11	  country?: string
12	  countryCode?: string
13	  region?: string
14	  latitude?: string
15	  longitude?: string
16	  timezone?: string
17	}
18	
19	const COUNTRY_NAMES: Record<string, string> = {
20	  US: 'United States',
21	  GB: 'United Kingdom',
22	  DE: 'Germany',
23	  FR: 'France',
24	  JP: 'Japan',
25	  CN: 'China',
26	  IN: 'India',
27	  BR: 'Brazil',
28	  AU: 'Australia',
29	  CA: 'Canada',
30	}
31	
32	function extractGeo(headers: Headers): Geo {
33	  const city = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-city') || undefined
34	  const countryCode =
35	    headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country') ||
36	    headers.get('cf-ipcountry') ||
37	    undefined
38	  const region = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country-region') || undefined
39	  const latitude = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-latitude') || undefined
40	  const longitude = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-longitude') || undefined
41	  const timezone = headers.get('x-vercel-ip-timezone') || undefined
42	
43	  const country = countryCode
44	    ? COUNTRY_NAMES[countryCode.toUpperCase()] || undefined
45	    : undefined
46	
47	  return {
48	    city,
49	    country,
50	    countryCode,
51	    region,
52	    latitude,
53	    longitude,
54	    timezone,
55	  }
56	}
57	
58	export function geo(req: { headers: Headers }): Geo {
59	  return extractGeo(req.headers)
60	}
61	
62	export function geoFromHeaders(headers: Headers): Geo {
63	  return extractGeo(headers)
64	}
65	
66	export function isInRegion(geoData: Geo, regions: string[]): boolean {
67	  if (!geoData.countryCode) {
68	    return false
69	  }
70	  const code = geoData.countryCode.toUpperCase()
71	  return regions.some((r) => r.toUpperCase() === code)
72	}
73	
74	export function geoDistance(
75	  lat1: string,
76	  lon1: string,
77	  lat2: string,
78	  lon2: string
79	): number {
80	  const R = 6371 // Earth's radius in kilometers
81	  const toRad = (deg: number) => (deg * Math.PI) / 180
82	
83	  const φ1 = toRad(parseFloat(lat1))
84	  const φ2 = toRad(parseFloat(lat2))
85	  const Δφ = toRad(parseFloat(lat2) - parseFloat(lat1))
86	  const Δλ = toRad(parseFloat(lon2) - parseFloat(lon1))
87
